name: From Alborge to Cinco Olivas — circular by the Río Ebro
name_original: De Alborge a Cinco Olivas — circular por el Río Ebro
region_intro: Hiking around Alborge offers routes through the flat terrain of the Ebro River basin. The landscape is characterized by agricultural fields and riverside paths, with minimal elevation changes. Many trails follow sections of the historic Way of St. James, providing cultural context to the natural surroundings. The region's hiking trails are generally accessible, featuring wide paths suitable for various activity levels. Best hiking trails around Alborge The most popular hiking route is Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route follows a section of the Way of St. James near the Rueda Monastery. Another top favourite among local hikers is From Alborge to Cinco Olivas — circular by the Río Ebro, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This circular route explores the area along the Río Ebro, offering views of the river and surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Way of St. James: Sástago Loop, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through the flat terrain of the Way of St. James, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Hiking around Alborge is defined by riverside paths, sections of the Way of St. James, and open agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy and moderate routes. The routes in Alborge are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from one review. More than a few hikers have used komoot to explore Alborge's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alborge?
A: There are 4 hiking trails documented around Alborge on komoot, offering a variety of experiences through the Ebro River basin.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Alborge?
A: Hiking around Alborge primarily features flat terrain within the Ebro River basin. You'll encounter agricultural fields and riverside paths, with minimal elevation changes, making for generally accessible walks.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Alborge?
A: Yes, Alborge offers several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167308240/cami-de-sant-jaume-and-gr-99-loop-from-rueda-monastery" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí de Sant Jaume and GR 99 Loop from Rueda Monastery&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.4 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167351643/way-of-st-james-sastago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way of St. James: Sástago Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option at 5.0 km.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alborge?
A: Yes, Alborge has several circular routes. You can explore the area along the Río Ebro with the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167329538/from-alborge-to-cinco-olivas-circular-by-the-rio-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Alborge to Cinco Olivas — circular by the Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, or try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alborge loop from Alborge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical lengths of hiking trails in Alborge?
A: The hiking trails in Alborge vary in length. You'll find routes ranging from approximately 5 km, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167351643/way-of-st-james-sastago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way of St. James: Sástago Loop&lt;/a&gt;, up to about 11.5 km for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alborge loop from Alborge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Alborge?
A: Many trails follow sections of the historic Way of St. James, offering cultural context. You can also enjoy views of the Ebro River and surrounding landscapes. Nearby attractions include the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sástago&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Tambor of Sástago&lt;/a&gt;, and scenic spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Meanders of the Ebro River and Sástago&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or natural features to explore?
A: Yes, you can find several viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Meanders of the Ebro River and Sástago&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning views of the river's winding path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another great spot to take in the scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6212554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín River and surroundings&lt;/a&gt; also offer natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Alborge?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the accessible paths, the peaceful riverside scenery, and the cultural significance of walking sections of the Way of St. James.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Alborge suitable for families?
A: Given the flat terrain and minimal elevation changes, many of the trails in Alborge are generally accessible and suitable for various activity levels, including families looking for easy and moderate walks. The wide paths make them comfortable for a leisurely outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Alborge?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, the natural and open character of the agricultural fields and riverside paths in Alborge generally makes them suitable for walks with dogs. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and follow local etiquette.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alborge?
A: The Ebro River basin can experience warm summers. Spring and autumn generally offer more comfortable temperatures for hiking, with pleasant weather for exploring the riverside paths and agricultural landscapes. Winter can also be suitable, though days are shorter.
tips: Cinco Olivas is a town that is located in one of the meanders of the Ebro River and that forms the set of three municipalities in these surroundings of the river. The Iglesia de Santiago is located in the old center of the town.
Beautiful locality at the foot of the Ebro river itself and which has interesting points such as the Mirador de las Tres aguas, north of the town.
